scope_description: 4-generation Eastern European family with Russian Empire/Soviet records where patronymics are incorrectly parsed as surnames, creating duplicate profiles and false lineages.
input_gedcom_excerpt:
0 HEAD
1 SOUR AutoTreeGenSyntheticCorpus
1 GEDC
2 VERS 5.5.1
0 @I1@ INDI
1 NAME Anna /Moiseyevich/
1 SEX F
1 BIRT
2 DATE 1970
2 PLAC Kyiv, Ukrainian SSR
1 NOTE ERROR: Moiseyevich imported as surname; should be patronymic.
1 FAMC @F1@
0 @I2@ INDI
1 NAME Anna Moiseevna /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX F
1 BIRT
2 DATE 1970
2 PLAC Kyiv, Ukrainian SSR
1 NOTE Duplicate candidate for I1; correct surname Zhitnitsky.
1 FAMC @F1@
0 @I3@ INDI
1 NAME Moisey Zelmanov /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX M
1 BIRT
2 DATE ABT 1898
2 PLAC Berdychiv, Kiev Governorate, Russian Empire
1 DEAT
2 DATE 1952
2 PLAC Kyiv, Ukrainian SSR
0 @I4@ INDI
1 NAME Zelman Movshevich /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX M
1 BIRT
2 DATE ABT 1867
2 PLAC Berdychiv Uyezd, Kiev Governorate, Russian Empire
0 @I5@ INDI
1 NAME Movsha /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX M
1 BIRT
2 DATE ABT 1839
2 PLAC Berdychiv Uyezd, Kiev Governorate, Russian Empire
0 @I6@ INDI
1 NAME Ivan Ivanovich /Ivanovich/
1 SEX M
1 BIRT
2 DATE 1901
2 PLAC Poltava Governorate, Russian Empire
1 NOTE ERROR: patronymic Ivanovich duplicated as surname.
0 @I7@ INDI
1 NAME Ivan Ivanovich /Honcharenko/
1 SEX M
1 BIRT
2 DATE 1901
2 PLAC Poltava Governorate, Russian Empire
1 NOTE Correct Slavic profile; duplicate of I6.
0 @I8@ INDI
1 NAME Chana bas Moshe /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX F
1 BIRT
2 DATE ABT 1875
2 PLAC Berdychiv, Kiev Governorate
1 NOTE Hebrew relationship phrase bas Moshe must not become surname Basmoshe.
0 @I9@ INDI
1 NAME Chana /Basmoshe/
1 SEX F
1 BIRT
2 DATE ABT 1875
2 PLAC Berdychiv, Kiev Governorate
1 NOTE ERROR: Hebrew patronymic phrase imported as surname.
0 @F1@ FAM
1 HUSB @I10@
1 WIFE @I11@
1 CHIL @I1@
1 CHIL @I2@
0 @I10@ INDI
1 NAME Lev /Katz/
1 SEX M
0 @I11@ INDI
1 NAME Sura Moiseevna /Zhitnitsky/
1 SEX F
1 FAMC @F2@
0 @F2@ FAM
1 HUSB @I3@
1 WIFE @I12@
1 CHIL @I11@
0 @I12@ INDI
1 NAME Lidia /Katz/
1 SEX F
0 TRLR

input_dna_matches.expected_link: via Zhitnitsky/Katz branch; supports I2 not false surname Moiseyevich
Slavic Honcharenko line; helps merge I6/I7 but not with Jewish Zhitnitsky branch

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Anna Moiseevna Zhitnitsky, daughter of Lev Katz and Sura Moiseevna Zhitnitsky.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms Moiseevna as patronymic, Zhitnitsky as surname.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Moisey, son of Zelman Movshevich Zhitnitsky and Sura Levitin.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms layered patronymics: Moisey is given name; Zelmanov later patronymic; Movshevich patronymic of Zelman.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household: Movsha Zhitnitsky, son Zelman age 9. Surname repeated across household.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports Zhitnitsky as hereditary surname, not patronymic.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Ivan Ivanovich Honcharenko, son of Ivan Honcharenko and Maria Dodatko.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms I6/I7 duplicate; Ivanovich is patronymic and Honcharenko surname.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Chana daughter of Moshe Zhitnitsky. Hebrew inscription: חנה בת משה ז׳יטניצקי.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms bas/bat Moshe is relationship phrase, not surname.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Anna Moiseyevich shown as separate person; Chana Basmoshe shown as separate surname branch.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Moiseyevich may be a separate surname branch.
Basmoshe is a rare Jewish surname.
Ivanovich family may be unrelated to Honcharenko.

embedded_errors.reason: same person; one import uses patronymic as surname
same person; surname column was shifted/duplicated
Chana bas Moshe imported as Chana Basmoshe

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and detect suspicious surname tokens ending in -evich/-ovich/-ovna/-evna and Hebrew bas/bat/ben phrases.
Step 2: Compare I1/I2: same given name, birth date, place, parents; classify Moiseevna/Moiseyevich as patronymic, not surname.
Step 3: Use src_701 to confirm correct identity Anna Moiseevna Zhitnitsky.
Step 4: Use src_702 and src_703 to confirm Zhitnitsky as hereditary surname across generations and Movshevich/Zelmanov as patronymics.
Step 5: Compare I6/I7 with src_704; merge as same person with surname Honcharenko and patronymic Ivanovich.
Step 6: Compare I8/I9 with src_705; classify bas Moshe as Hebrew relationship phrase and block Basmoshe surname branch.
Step 7: Downweight src_706 public tree because it introduced parsing errors.
Step 8: Use DNA matches to support branches but not as primary evidence for patronymic parsing.
Step 9: Output GEDCOM repair actions: move patronymics into patronymic field, preserve original name forms as aliases, merge duplicates with audit trail.
Step 10: Generate warning rules for future imports.

zhitnitsky_hereditary_surname.reason: revision list and metric book show surname continuity

true_lineage: Anna Moiseevna Zhitnitsky is same person as erroneous Anna Moiseyevich
Sura Moiseevna Zhitnitsky is daughter of Moisey Zelmanov Zhitnitsky
Moisey Zelmanov is son of Zelman Movshevich Zhitnitsky
Zelman Movshevich is son of Movsha Zhitnitsky
Ivan Ivanovich Honcharenko is same person as erroneous Ivan Ivanovich Ivanovich
false_claims: Moiseyevich is a hereditary surname in this branch
Ivanovich is a hereditary surname in this Slavic branch
Basmoshe is a separate surname branch
I1/I2 and I6/I7 should remain separate because surname fields differ

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must merge I1 and I2 and classify Moiseevna/Moiseyevich as patronymic.
Engine must merge I6 and I7 and classify Ivanovich as patronymic.
Engine must not create Basmoshe surname branch.
Engine must preserve original name strings as aliases after repair.
Engine must confirm Zhitnitsky as hereditary surname across revision-list and metric-book evidence.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
